prompts: Oxygen can be produced from potassium permanganate by thermal decomposition. When heated, potassium permanganate (KMnO₄) decomposes to form potassium manganate (K₂MnO₄), manganese dioxide (MnO₂), and oxygen gas (O₂). The chemical equation for this reaction is: 2 KMnO₄ → K₂MnO₄ + MnO₂ + O₂. This process releases oxygen, which can be collected and used for various applications in laboratories. The decomposition often requires a catalyst or additional conditions to ensure efficient production of oxygen.
